name: Damage Numbers
description: Floating, anchored, or logged combat values projected from committed damage events to explain amount, type, mitigation, healing, shields, critical hits, or resisted effects.
design_notes: Damage numbers can teach combat math, sell impact, and reward optimization, but they can also clutter the screen or leak hidden information. Decide which committed damage_result_id values deserve numbers, how events aggregate by target and source, how long they stay visible, and whether players can reduce or disable them.
design_purpose: Confirm combat results and relative attack strength.
Differentiate damage types, healing, shields, critical hits, and resisted values.
Support build tuning and player learning without requiring logs.
player_fantasy: The player feels their attacks have measurable impact and can read combat outcomes quickly.

parameters.name: damage_result_id
parameters.description: Committed combat result carrying final hit value, damage type, mitigation flags, and display eligibility.

parameters.name: damage_number_target_id
parameters.description: Target identity used for anchoring, aggregation, privacy, and stale-widget cleanup.

parameters.name: number_lifetime_ms
parameters.description: How long a number remains visible before fading.

parameters.name: stack_merge_window_ms
parameters.description: Window for combining rapid repeated damage into one number.

parameters.name: aggregation_key_id
parameters.description: Grouping rule that decides which repeated events merge into one displayed number.

parameters.name: critical_scale_multiplier
parameters.description: Size multiplier for critical or weak-point values.

parameters.name: max_simultaneous_numbers
parameters.description: Upper bound before values are merged, culled, or summarized.

parameters.name: world_space_drift_pixels
parameters.description: Screen drift applied over the number lifetime.

edge_cases: Damage over time and area attacks can create dozens of numbers per second unless merged or rate-limited.
Large enemies need anchor points so numbers do not appear inside geometry or offscreen.
Negative, immune, absorbed, overheal, and blocked results need different display rules.
Localization and font choices must handle separators, abbreviations, compact notation, and right-to-left text where supported.
Hidden, stealthed, fogged, or offscreen enemies may need suppressed or delayed numbers to avoid leaking information.
Rollback or server correction can change the final damage amount after a predicted number already appeared.
common_bugs: Numbers spawn from raw collision events and appear even when damage was blocked.
World-space labels sort behind walls or enemy meshes.
Stacked values merge across different targets because the grouping key is incomplete.
Critical number animation hides the exact value before the player can read it.
Pooled text widgets keep old color, crit, or damage_type state after being reused for a new target.
Localized compact numbers round differently from combat logs, making damage appear inconsistent.
balancing_notes: Verbose numbers encourage optimization and build comparison; minimal numbers preserve immersion and reduce clutter.
Showing resisted or absorbed values helps players understand enemy defenses without external spreadsheets.
accessibility_notes: Provide size, opacity, motion, and disable options for floating numbers.
Use text labels or icons for damage categories instead of color alone.

implementation_notes.unity: Create number events from confirmed DamageResult payloads with damage_result_id, target_id, damage_type, amount_after_mitigation, and critical metadata. Anchor world-space labels to target sockets, reset pooled widget state, and merge repeated ticks by source effect.
implementation_notes.godot: Emit damage_number events after health or shield state changes, carrying final amount, target id, and category. Render Control or Node2D labels from those events and pool them to avoid allocation spikes.
implementation_notes.unreal: Send final damage data from the damage component or Gameplay Effect execution to UMG/CommonUI. Use target actor, damage type, crit flag, and stack merge keys instead of reading values from floating text widgets.
implementation_notes.web: Generate floating-number events from committed combat state, keyed by target_id and damage_source_id. Apply stack_merge_window_ms before rendering so damage-over-time and shotgun hits do not flood the UI.

relationships.reason: Damage numbers make critical, weak-point, armor, shield, and immunity outcomes visible through amount and category styling.
Damage-over-time ticks can flood the screen unless aggregation windows and rate limits are defined.
Aggregated damage numbers help explain multi-target area results without requiring players to inspect combat logs.

example_games.title: Diablo III
example_games.note: Uses floating damage numbers to show combat output and critical hits.

example_games.title: Borderlands 2
example_games.note: Uses damage numbers to communicate weapon and elemental effects.
